storyboardTitle: May 12, 2022 Derecho — Live Severe Weather Simulator

steps.title: Simulation Begins
narrative: It is 3:00 PM CDT. The atmosphere is primed, and attention is turning to storm development west of Sioux Falls.
Use this opening step to orient the class before the severe weather phase begins.

steps.title: Storms Begin to Organize
narrative: Radar echoes are becoming more coherent west of the James Valley.
This is the transition from background setup to storm mode.

event.headline: Storms Intensifying
event.message: Radar returns are beginning to consolidate west of Sioux Falls.

steps.title: Warnings Enter the Story
narrative: Severe thunderstorm warnings begin to matter more as storms approach the Sioux Falls region.
The simulator should now feel more urgent and more real-time.

event.headline: Severe Thunderstorm Warning
event.message: A warning polygon is now in play. Watch radar and the warning edge together.

steps.title: Live Doppler Moment
narrative: This is a strong teaching moment to toggle on the Live Doppler sweep and focus attention on the storm core.
Students should feel like they are watching a live television weather cut-in.

event.headline: Live Doppler On
event.message: Turn on the sweep and narrate what is changing storm by storm.

steps.title: Tornado Warning Flashpoint
narrative: This is the big interruption point of the simulator.
Pause the timeline, let the alert breathe on screen, and talk through why this moment matters.

event.headline: Tornado Warning
event.message: Pause the simulator here and examine the warning polygon and radar presentation together.

steps.title: Damage Reports Begin
narrative: Storm reports now add reality to the radar presentation.
This is where the event starts feeling broader than a single warned storm.

event.headline: Damage Reports Increasing
event.message: Wind damage reports are starting to confirm what radar has been suggesting.

steps.title: Line Matures
narrative: The storm is evolving toward a more organized damaging wind event.
Use this step to explain how a line changes the hazard footprint.

steps.title: Lightning Peaks
narrative: If lightning data is available, this is a natural point to turn it on for extra realism.
Otherwise, keep the focus on radar, warnings, and reports.

event.headline: Lightning Increasing
event.message: If enabled, lightning adds another real-time layer to the experience.

steps.title: Aftermath Window
narrative: The most intense phase is moving east, but impacts remain.
Wrap the lesson by connecting radar evolution, warnings, and damage reports to the bigger derecho story.
